title: Instrumentality Whose Fault is It
wikitext:
[]: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 5:00 pm SPARTAN117CJL wrote: Technically, Shinji is the Savior, if not for him we'd still be floating around in instrumentality.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 12, 2007 5:55 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Did she?

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 6:08 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: Yes. Kaworu proves this.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Wed Dec 12, 2007 6:32 pm I always got the sense that Shinji was just a mere pawn. It didn't matter who it was in Unit-01, just as long as they said "YES KILL THEM ALL." (Cries of "NO SHIT REALLY!?" are hurled at chee)

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 6:37 pm Shinji was a pawn "in the grand scale of Seele's plans for Instrumentality and whatnot". But in the sense that Yui and Rei took control of things, I do not believe he was any longer a pawn. Here are the two people who love and care for him the most; Why would they ask him what he wanted and then not grant it? To me, that just seems silly.

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Wed Dec 12, 2007 6:42 pm Well, we don't really know that. @Chee. On the one hand: Shinji was INSIDE shogouki when 3I was instigated. Shogouki was the focal point of the Instrumentality process. So maybe, the will of the pilot, whoever it was, controlled 3I. On the other hand: Rei felt something for Shinji. Not love, but a connection. This is something we know, and it's possible the bit that was Rei inside Lilith gave control over to Shinji instead of doing it herself, because she knew Shinji. We're never going to really be able to come to a consensus about this, because we AREN'T GNR, and we'll never get inside GNR's head. Really, Instrumentality is Dr. Katsuragi's fault for finding Adam in the first place, and Gehirn/Nerv/Seele's fault for building the Evangelion units and keeping Lilith around.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 6:54 pm Shiro wrote: We're never going to really be able to come to a consensus about this, because we AREN'T GNR, and we'll never get inside GNR's head.

[]:             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Dec 12, 2007 7:05 pm Shinji may have turned them into to tang...but is he REALLY the one one who responsible for them becoming tang. As Maya herself said..."This is the answer I was looking for...Death" Shinji had the balls to go on living (though he did need a pep talk) no one else did, I think we should leave it at that.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 12, 2007 7:55 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: I think that was a slightly different situation. Besides, considering we don't know what was going through his noggin' during his trip down to TD, we don't know all the reasons why he did what he did.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:08 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: That's idiotic to say the least. It's your choice in the end. You have the option to completely ignore my input if you so choose. I can't make you do something.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Themaninblack » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:20 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: You know why I hate this whole idea? Because you know good and well that Anno wants us to focus on the idea of Shinji choosing to end it all and not bail him out on any narrative technicality. He gave him the choice, as literally and symbolically as he could. GNR is simply there as a big narrative device to carry out that decision. By placing the blame on GNR, you're turning the focus away from what's important (Shinji's decision) onto some rather arbitrary narrative device that Anno chose and that's just not right.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:30 pm Themaninblack wrote: Shinji choose life in the end and just like how you said Shinji would be able to plead ignorance, I'd think he'd be able to plead temporary insanity.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:35 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Let's face it, Shinji's seen enough stuff happen to plead ignorance in this case. I will not believe that was innocent in knowing roughly what would happen by saying "everyone can just die".

[]:             Quote Post by Wevangeliwon » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:47 pm Oh man, this is a tough call. The two it shifts on is Shinji and Rei. The fact is Rei had the free will to choose whether she wanted to become omnipotent or not. Kaworu proved this. The fact she did choose means she shifted power onto Shogoki, which was the epicenter of 3I. As for Shinji, he was very mentally unstable at the time. He was part of the center and had the mentality of 'let them all die'. If Shinji was what he was like . . . oh, say, just before episode sixteen when he was somewhat happy, I don't think 3I would have happened the way it did. The fact was at the time he was unstable. He was given the powers of a God, if I could put it that way. He destroyed what our world was and then chose life, meaning humanity could begin anew. If I had to choose who was responsible, though, I would say it . . . damn, this is difficult. Rei probably knew Ikari was mentally unstable, but he did initiate 3I. Like Rei, I could argue Shinji had free will. I shift blame onto Shinji. Oh, slightly OT: when I was watching EoE, when Fuyutsuki says that their fate lies in the hands of Shinji, if I was a bridge bunny, I would be thinking: "...we're fucked."

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 8:50 pm Good post on the more neutral side of things Wevangeliwon. I begrudgingly tend to agree that blame lies on BOTH parties as well. But I put so much more blame on Shinji solely because someone had to make the choice, and it's clear that GNR gave him that choice. Wevangeliwon wrote: when Fuyutsuki says that their fate lies in the hands of Shinji, if I was a bridge bunny, I would be thinking: "...we're fucked."

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Semisubtle » Wed Dec 12, 2007 10:29 pm Anonymous_Evafan wrote: She wanted him to decide and so he did.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 10:32 pm Indeed, to gloss over Shinji's physical powerlessness is to to completely miss what Anno's saying in that the choices we make is the most powerful tool we have, and since it is all we have control over, we should choose wisely (unlike the guy in The Last Crusade. He chose.... poorly).

[]:             Quote Post by Shiro » Thu Dec 13, 2007 12:43 am Look at it this way. If it was all up to Giant Naked Rei, then why even bother asking Shinji? That whole scene would be pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by jenova7 » Thu Dec 13, 2007 12:47 am Shiro said : Look at it this way. If it was all up to Giant Naked Rei, then why even bother asking Shinji? That whole scene would be pointless

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Dec 13, 2007 1:57 am Shiro wrote: Look at it this way. If it was all up to Giant Naked Rei, then why even bother asking Shinji? That whole scene would be pointless.
summary:
[]: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Dec 12, 2007 5:00 pm SPARTAN117CJL wrote: Technically, Shinji is the Savior, if not for him we'd still be floating around in instrumentality.

[]: Re: Instrumentality: Whose Fault is It?             Quote Post by Anonymous_Evafan » Wed Dec 12, 2007 5:55 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Did she?

[]: Re: Instru
